channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Theyre ending the Chainlink BUILD program — 57 replies — 228.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Theyre ending the Chainlink BUILD program

Open thread • Replies: 57 • Updated: 2026-06-27 20:51 UTC

OP

LMAO!!!!!

https://chain.link/blog/build-program-evolution

Early replies (first 3)

62417511
xrp is the chosen coin…it’s undeniable at this point

62417512
Come on man, this shit still spamming after 8 years? Just give it up. There are thousands of other coins.

62417514
"Chainlink Labs is transitioning from payments made in project tokens to focusing on commercial agreements involving fees paid in LINK or other liquid assets that can be readily converted to LINK."

Build members will buy my bags?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62418519
being in the usa i couldn't get those rewards anyway
and no i didn't want to risk anything with a VPN

62418676
>>62417503
after the claim shitshow its not surprising
i didnt even bother claiming those shitcoins

62418693
>>62418149
Who cares? Dont get me wrong I see the point of staking in theory… plug your 10k link in there and make 500k/year or whatever. My point is it doesn’t matter if you take. Cuz you got 10 million whether you stake or not

62418828
NIGGER!

62418829
>>62418149
How will you steak without LPL?
Think long and hard about it

62418846
>>62418693
It's a fair point buuuuut think of it like you get a high yield on tcp/ip or the global water reserve, resources which are highly necessary and consistently increase in their value and what it costs to utilize them, and are not going away within your lifetime or the next 10-100+. Why even care about putting your money elsewhere? The opportunity to invest in something like Chainlink - core infrastructure with insane value capture potential, has never really existed before. The closest thing maybe would be investing in AMZN prior to cloud services, but this is actually a way bigger scale because data/compute is an infant compared to the system of MONEY.

It's an insane long play but it has legs still, given all the adoption we've seen.

I think it's a very real possibility most early linkies crash out having made nothing or lost money even after 10+ years and then we legitimately see insane levels of adoption start happening and the token price go wildly parabolic. That doesn't mean you wouldn't still have been better off selling at a $30-50 pre 2027 high, opportunity cost is real.

62418853
>>62418519
bro you literally have zero risk now, they're shutting the program down, just grab your tokens and sell them

>t. US citizen who cashed out all tokens early, still stakes, zero repercussions

62418997
this is quite the embarrassing end to this chapter of being a linky

62419194
>>62418503
>people on 4chan are super fearful for half a year now
>its peak fear
>half manufactured
I guess... im gonna get even more linkies

62419208
>>62417888
>3,000 link at $1,000 is 3 million
$5 is a lot more likely. Maybe even lower.

62419298
>>62418846
Adoption that has no impact on the token value whatsoever. I've been blocked twice by the Chainlink intern handling the twitter account for calling them out. Will continue to do so once I create another account. Sergey probably has a security detail like some high level politician by now.

62419349
Arguably was the most logical choice in the first place. Although my God are the optics terrible. Who the fuck is running their marketing and communications.

Chris Barrett and Rory and chainlink God need to shut the fuck up desu. I still get they don't want to talk about the token which is fair enough things aren't locked in stone legally and cunts talking about other tokens do xyz need to understand the way chainlink labs operates is still centralised so they are still open to legal scrutiny.

The fact they can't say some basics like link staking next iteration is being worked on is surprising. I hate how coy they act like heh we just dump tokens on binance whoever buys it we don't know as if people didn't fund their entire company.

Governments globally are to blame allowing any token sales to not have the same protection as stocks imo. Digital commodities my ass when they can press a button and mint tokens out of thin air.

62419353
it sucks, but please don't sell, ok? please

62419373
We hadn't heard from it in a year and in something like 3 years of BUILD we effectively had one drop from all the shitcoins getting subsidized services from us. I never even bothered claiming.

62419589
>>62417503
BULLISH

62419602
>>62417888
>3,000 link at $1,000 is 3 million
Linkies are mentally retarded handicapped baggies kek

Your shitcoin is trading at $7 and you're poor

62419628
>>62417503
This is a GOOD choice, but its LATE. Why the fuck does it take ~5 years to come to shit conclusion that you dont want 1000's of garbage tokens?

Why the fuck now?

At this point, the critics were right, the only way to win with LINK is to SHORT IT.

62419638
>>62417511

Are you people all mad. Here’s some assets since new year. Why the FUCK would you be in Link or XRP. Look at how much money you’ve fucking lost potentially. Are you actually retarded. Even gold only lost 5%

62419676
>>62417503
Not only are they not ending Build, they're making it all about the Link token.
Build payments and rewards used to be in member tokens, now they will either be in Link or be converted to Link.

It's right there in black and white, you illiterate brown.

62419679
>>62419349
Look at our little armchair CEO over here. How's your tech company doing?

62419687
>>62419676
But they're taking the rewards away from stakers. It's all going to the Reserve.

I think everyone was hoping staking and thr distrobution of protocol revenue would all be handled programmatically (Payment Abstraction seemed to be a step towards this) but instead everything is routed through Chainlink Labs.
Initially I thought the BUILD rewards would be auto-converted to LINK and then contribute to the staking APY. Instead we're stuck at Staking 0.2 and BUILD goes to Reserve. Wtf.

62419694
>>62419676
Well, unless you actually receive additional LINK as a staker, it's completely irrelevant. Who knows how long they will keep this pacifier staking running anyway? They can pull the plug at any time. No consequences as it has been clear as day that this whole paper staking experiment was made up to shut up the critics. Didn't last long though...

62419707
>>62419687
>they're taking the rewards away from stakers
Build rewards were never going 100% to stakers.

>It's all going to the Reserve.
The cap in OP clearly says otherwise.

Learn to read, brown.

>>62419694
>unless you actually receive additional LINK as a staker, it's completely irrelevant.
Tell it to the fuddies howling about how they don't want "random shitcoins".

62419888
So the build rewards that were hyped (I remember the retard with the spreadsheet valuations lmfao) turned out to be a flop and worth pennies.
Now these literal pennies will be converted and go to the already small reserve with 0 price impact. While they put in 1 millions worth of tokens per week they still dump 8% of the supply per year.

Inb4 the sperg comes in "tokens unlocks are not sales" they create sell pressure , nigger.

62419949
>>62419349
>The fact they can't say some basics like link staking next iteration is being worked on is surprising
What if they don’t say it because it is not being worked on? Why draw attention to it?

62419959
>>62417503
Nomore free rides for build startups, now they need to pay?

Not sure if bullish or bearish desu

62420074
>>62419194
I'm a fuddie but you are right. If you're a holder then acoomulate more and lower your price targets next cycle.

62420086
>>62417888
Checked, counting the chickens before they hatch

62420241
>>62419888
Checked, and correct. We are fucked, well it was nice thinking i would get rich one day

62420825
>>62419888
>>62420241

You faggots are like the riffraff who like to go to the casino to play your game of choice but the motivation to get you out the door is some stupid fucking promotional thing. Like you like to play poker or blackjack, and you are even good at these games, but you only decide to go cuz there’s some free slot play for $10 for signing up for a their casino card.

It’s like you faggots think that these build rewards would make up half your profit from investing in link. Link goes to $500 and you have 5 million dollars, and your expecting some bullshit build token to make you 2.5 million dollars also?

item.title: WARNING: RIPPLE XRP ***EXTREME*** SUPER EMERGENCY!!! — 14 replies — 48.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
WARNING: RIPPLE XRP ***EXTREME*** SUPER EMERGENCY!!!

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-06-27 20:42 UTC

OP

Insiders at Ripple officially speculate the XRP going to moon very high soon with the clarity act after the 4th of July, according to rumors, analysts say. This is huge and **MASSIVE** news for XRP and the cryptocurrency community as a whole.

You. Are. Not. Bullish. Enough.

Early replies (first 3)

62420069
>>62420066
shut the fuck up

62420100
>>62420066
XRP is a slush fund for banks. That is why they partnered with it.

62420110
So bullish

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62420119
Yes

62420157
Alright why is it pumping so hard?

62420174
>>62420157
It’s only up about 2% at the moment. Even 5% up isn’t much.

62420277
>>62420174
Bullish

62420439
BOOOOOOOOOOOM

62420471
>>62420066
XRP died in 2024

62420479
>>62420066
I sold this scam the last pump attempt.

Thanks retards

62420480
>>62420471
>ATH was after 2024
>It died before that

Based retard

62420531
>>62420066
HOLY FXCKING BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62420698
>>62420110
Fkn bullish

62420798
Please stay in your containment thread. Thank you for your consideration.

item.title: >only down 4% from its ipo, which is very reasonable >now we know the price floor is $150 now is the time to buy SPCX — 40 replies — 38.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
>only down 4% from its ipo, which is very reasonable >now we know the price floor is $150 now is the time to buy SPCX

Open thread • Replies: 40 • Updated: 2026-06-27 19:57 UTC

OP

>only down 4% from its ipo, which is very reasonable
>now we know the price floor is $150
now is the time to buy SPCX

Early replies (first 3)

62408493
>>62408480
Bagholder cope.

62408507
>>62408493
No cope saar only gains to be made! Please buy more stocks from this very profitable company! Time is limited for maximum gains must hurry before money train takes off!

62408519
>>62408480
>>only down 4% from its ipo,
kek about to 50% in 6 months

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62411812
>>62409551
>>62409673
>>62409699
>>62409872
150 is the floor

62411816
>>62408480
FLOOR BROS
WE'RE TESTING AGAIN

62411883
>>62409551
Yep. The insider shares haven't even been unlocked. Why is he in such a rush? Just wait. Massive supply coming in a couple of months. Michael Howell already says marginal liquidity in financial markets is lower.

Thus, SUPPLY++ & DEMAND??? = Likely lower price.

62414040
>>62408480
should I buy in now? I want to hold some before starship 13

62414121
>>62408480
with only 5% of the float available or w/e. holy shit yall are fucked when it opens up

62414137
>>62408480
Melon got re-bogged and fattened which he only does when things are going badly for him
2x short because of this

62414157
>>62408480
>We privatized NASA and now we want you to pay us to see Hollywood for scientists productions
Yeah, nah. I saw Kubrick admit to faking the moon landing.

62414178
>>62408480
There's genuinely not enough data to make a principled model of it. However the only thing not hypey about SpaceX is StarLink, which is a genuine business. Merging xAI into SpaceX however just tied a 1 ton ball and chain around it, because it's an insatiable blackhole of capex with no clear upside.

62415681
>>62414137
he was spending too much time being a master of POE2 instead of going to the gym. he'll get back on the horse.

62415707
This thread didn't age well.

62415715
>>62415707
$150 is still the floor. it'll be $300 by hanukkah

62415763
>>62411812
Holy baggie, this garbage isn't worth a fraction of what it is, why would your delusional number be even close to the floor

62416005
>>62408480
90x earnings is INSANE. Investing in this is a sure marker you are retarded and willful exit liquidity. Equivalent to buying Worldcomm with the exception that Space X probably has a longterm future, but is at absolute bubble top with an ABSURD valuation and no course to revenues in the near future, kept aloft by virtue of its multiple rules changed inclusion in indices receiving passive flows and retail hype that has come and gone.

62416247
>>62416005
>>62415763
it's already up for the day!

62416343
>>62416005
>Investing in this is a sure marker you are retarded and willful exit liquidity

while true, never underestimate the retardation of the retail masses

62416471
>>62408480
Keep buy Bagholders, keep buying!

62416737
>>62408480
I haven't seen a single bull "case" that has used numbers for buying Spcx. Meanwhile all Bears have to do is look at that those Quarterly and Y/Y financials directly below OP's image they were conveniently edited out.

62416873
>>62415707
Yep people of /biz/ seem to be unable to value this asset correctly. The SpaceMarines are back!!

62417077
doubters in shambles

62417086
AI FUCKING DATA CENTERS IN SPACE!

62417121
>>62408480
IT JUST KEEPS TUMBLIMG DOWN TUMBLIMG DOWN
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oIscL-Bjsq4

BAGGIES ARE YOU OK
BAGGIES ARE YOU OK
ARE YOU OK BAGGIES


Did you guys manage to sell in thegreen be honest?

62417135
>>62408480
ITS OVER

62417520
>>62417077
Yep, truly in shambles. Another down day.

Even if SpaceX is an amazing compnay, entry point matters. SpaceX market cap at $1 trillion is when it starts to get somewhat attractive. Just be patient young Padawon.

62418371
>>62408480
>only down 4% from its ipo, which is very reasonable
okay so you expected 30% by now... or what..... what is this point? this isnt even cope it's just weird

62419735
>>62408480
The price floor was always $150.
If it's below that at (can't remember date) they can't sell more stock.
So it's got to be $150 by that date. Someone can find it. Don't care.

62419742
isn't most of retail locked in until precisely the point where early investors are legally allowed to sell and cash in at any price? oh yes they are.

62419746
>>62414178
the listing has a TAM of equivalent of the total value of the us economy, or the the money spent on food by the planet, every year, year on year, to pay for its ai services. are elons data centres worth more than the entire us economy, or the entire global food chain supply, now or in the future?

62419817
>>62419746
Qualifier.
Do I live in a machine in the future?

62419957
OP bought the top and is now panicking to sell the bottom

62420705
>>62419957
This is the vibe I'm getting. I really hope you guys didn't see the most obvious pump and dump scheme in history and still fall for it like an indian who can see the train coming yet still gets hit by it.

item.title: What's the fundamental bull case for this? — 5 replies — 16.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
What's the fundamental bull case for this?

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-06-27 20:41 UTC

OP

What's the fundamental bull case for this? What about USDT/USDC adoption? 2.5% APR seems nice too.
It's pretty cheap right now and I'm considering buying some.

Early replies (first 3)

62420784
>>62420744
it's all funny money, anon.

62420787
The bull case for the past 4-5 years has been that institutions will utilize ETH for the tokenization of assets like stocks.

Well, turns out the institutions decided to use ETH as a blueprint to create their own private smart contract platforms, while picking and choosing which features they want to keep. Their view of ETH is merely that of a public testing grounds for what works and what doesn't. It's grim

62420791
>>62420787
Damn

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62420794
>>62420791
Before you buy any, ask yourself why so many people from the Ethereum Foundation has been jumping ship recently. If the future was bright, would they do that?

62420797
Yall gots dat efferiumz?

item.title: Day 345 of snailposting every day until LINK is at $100 — 3 replies — 6.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Day 345 of snailposting every day until LINK is at $100

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-06-27 20:33 UTC

OP

Day 345 of snailposting every day until LINK is at $100

Early replies (first 3)

62420616
>>62420039
Still holding my (she/her) LINK, $100 LFG

62420730
20 days until this thread is spam and will be deleted

62420780
>>62420730
>20 days until this thread is spam and will be deleted

every shitling bread is spam
>rangeban india!!!!!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: how the FUCK do you get a job in this economy? — 37 replies — 78.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
how the FUCK do you get a job in this economy?

Open thread • Replies: 37 • Updated: 2026-06-27 20:31 UTC

OP

how the FUCK do you get a job in this economy?

Early replies (first 3)

62417182
know somebody

62417183
firm handshake

62417184
become a plumber

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62417357
pull yourself up by the bootstraps

62418447
learn to code welding robots

62418479
>>62417164
Pressure washer.
>>62417275
Same. I’m guessing it’s demoralizing bots.

62418486
Mind your fucking business. Do that for a few decades.

62418538
literally just get an entry level job at a big company like a bank and work your way up

I was a dropout neet in 2016 and I got a job at a mail room in a bank office part time making minimum wage. Made some moves up and now I'm a """"cyber security analyst"""" which is a bullshit laptop job.

62418632
>>62417275
ive never had a job in 10 years, i show up to interviews, awkwardly go through it, never hear back. I gave up desu. I have no idea how people get jobs or stay in them without burning out.

62418762
>>62417275
Yeah honestly it’s not hard, I have gone through 3 jobs in the last five years,
The unemployment rate is low too so it’s not like they have an excuse

Maybe they like just graduated high school or something

62418768
>>62417164
I work the opposite side. I'm an independent contractor, businesses apply to me to get me to work for them. I interview them and check their references and if they make the cut I'll go work for them. I have far more applications than I can ever work for, so it's never a problem finding work

62418796
>>62417270
Based. Postman is a close second
T. Postman

62418819
>>62417182
>>62417183
>>62417183
Do all of these one after another OP

62418886
>>62417275
Not finishing hs and being terminally unhireable with no contacts and demoralized for 2 decades will do it

62418899
>>62418796
Honestly my ideal job...being paid to walk. I also dont care about keeping it more than 5 years so im willing to fuck up someones dog if it came to that

62418900
i hate normies who just talk casually about how easy they get jobs. you people are monkeys. GIVE ME A JOB IF ITS SO EASY

62418901
>>62418900
Well nepotism still exist.

62418926
>>62418538
>literally just get an entry level job at a big company like a bank and work your way up
Is this before or after I give a firm handshake and hand them my resume?

62419033
>>62417184
Tick tock, tradie.

https://www.anthropic.com/research/project-fetch-phase-two

62419102
>>62418886
Bro, get your GED. And then go to trade school or something. You managed to get to high school, all you gotta do is finish. Getting hired in this era of extreme nepotism, on the other hand, I don't know. It's likely all online job listings are fake, as I was never hired through those (I have an actual email from a so-called Mary Perez from Burlington gloating about it them being fake). I just about managed to get hired at an ISP during a job fair after completing a networking certificate. All I had to do was pass their written test. Though, you really need to work your way asap towards a CCNA if you want to keep the job. Just need to follow Comptia's cert map. You could also go for trades like Electrician or Plumbing, which could lead to self-employment, but I can't help you with those.

62419300
>>62417164
nurses and care takers are important and needed.

office chair warming keyboard clackers are getting culled.

the world is healing

62419584
be good at what you do
im so good at what i do they gave me a car to get there and a paid commute because initially turned down the job because i didnt want to drive that far

62419594
>>62417164
You take what's being offered and figure it out from there. Get a factory job, a Walmart job, etc.

62419800
>>62418899
I'm approaching year 4 and I've only had two dog incidents. It's always the small dogs. Big dogs may bark if their owner isn't around but odds are they are well trained and you can tell them to sit and they listen. The supervisors just tells us not to even attempt delivery if there are dogs in a yard because they don't want to do paperwork for incidents. Also mixed feelings on this, my area is converting to community mailboxes so pretty much eliminating walking. I like driving around and it's super easy to deliver to one spot but I'll have to start doing more cardiovascular exercise outside of work.

62419806
>>62417164
Put nigger on your CV. Under experiences write police brutality. The femoids in HR will pay you to go work there.

62420387
>>62418796
I'm a train driver and feel like you guys work harder. Would love to be a postman on a postie bike if trains get automated here.

62420535
>>62419102
>It's likely all online job listings are fake
At least 90% are and the few remaining get hundreds of applicants so you're guaranteed to be competing with PHDs for 18 bucks an hour work.

62420580
>>62417275
Dont want to discourage anyone, maybe its just me.
i have 10 years in aerospace eng., took me 16 months to find a job. Have sent thousands of applications, hundreds of linkedin dms. Even some cold calls (actually cold calling worked best). Granted the new job is FAANG but requires relocation.

62420599
>>62417164
Be brown and pay an Indian college for a fake degree. You might be able to get a job at a Walmart then.

62420653
>>62418900
i think a big part of the sub culture of 4chan is introverts. you have to talk to people, like in real life. getting a mcjob trade job is a lot easier these days, getting a decent tech job not so much.

otherwise fake being an incompetent LGBTWFTBBQ, big companies love that shit

62420692
This is the new normal, I fear.

62420767
>>62417182
Truke

62420776
>>62417275
>I’m honestly confused how you guys have so much trouble.
14 years gap in my resume, it's the kind of gap you can't come back from

item.title: VOO and Chill — 110 replies — 38.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
VOO and Chill

Open thread • Replies: 110 • Updated: 2026-06-27 18:07 UTC

OP

The more I look into investing and compare the historical returns of certain ETFs / individual stocks it seems like the whole philosophy of “VOO and Chill” is equal parts really smart and retarded
It’s like a no brainer for money but even now as long as the market is in a growth period shouldn’t you at least do QQQ?
It seems so simple that’d you miss a lot of upside but the returns are honestly not bad and extremely consistent
How accurate is this assessment? It’s both so simple and guaranteed that everyone should it but also too safe so you end up missing out on more upside
I’m talking 5-20 years investing here not trading

Early replies (first 3)

62400515
Boomer funds are gay and lame

62400554
>>62400515
What qualifies as one
Why
What crypto do you want to shill

62400560
boglehead method is the tried and tested way, buy the market with an index fund that has a low fee, invest monthly, do it consistently for decades and you WILL be rich
but biz/anons don't care about being rich when they're older, they want to be rich NOW
greed is a deadly sin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62413963
If you don't just want to do VOO and chill only, just have a separate account at Vanguard that you treat like a savings account and deposit something there monthly to buy VOO or whatever. You'll be glad later on.

62414091
>>62412068
>checking your account daily
>investing only in one country

62414103
For me its 90% VOO
10% GPIQ
So i win even when I crab

62414142
>>62413705
>Felix and friends on youtube
>ex investment banker
>"Discover A Trading System That Makes Market Headlines Irrelevant"

62414162
>>62400493
Fuck VOO
>50% VT
>50% VTI
That's the real chill. Total U.S. investment is 81% of portfolio, you capture emerging markets, and some great companies you otherwise wouldn't have in VOO or VTI alone.

62414279
>>62414162
What’s the best way to compare the two? Track the CAGRs of VOO vs the average of VT and VTI combined

62414680
>>62414279
If you do that, its boomer tier analysis, otherwise that analysis would dictate to just buy VOO or QQQ overlooking the real value of VT/I. VT is used as a hedge if there's an exodus from U.S. markets in the next 5-10 years. VTI is used as a hedge against an exodus from bloated tech in the next 5-10 years but the U.S is still supreme (likely the case). When investment eventually settles from AI bloat and only 3-5 companies out of the current hundreds trying to pursue AI development come out on top, investment will switch into a different sector. With these etfs, you dont have to worry about what the next big thing is, which is why its the ultimate hold. Zero think. Comfy returns and they trail VOO by total return over 10 years 309% vs 325%, VT and VTI both have lowe p/e which makes the more value and less risk. Buy, hold, DRIP, and sell when you want to. This is the long end of my portfolio, and my 401k and IRAs. This is shit I am not touching for 30-40 years. Some people in my firm freak out over market turmoil, im zen, where one bucket empties, another fills for me.

62414768
>>62413705
>Felix and friends
pic is the better Felix

62414776
>>62414162
vti+vxus is more tax efficient

62414829
>>62414776
Barely, but VXUS does have the pas-through tax credit, something that amounts to $100 per $100k invested, which is better, plus you can set your parameters for how much you want allocated to international. But have you ever had to explain to a 24yo HR roastie to set up 15% portfolio allocation of VTIAX/VXUS?
>HALF HERE HALF THERE IT CANT BE THIS FUCKING HARD
The headache is not worth the hassle.

62415002
>>62410414
VOO is not 15%, its 10%
>since inception
I don't give a shit, its such a low sample that it's nearly statistically irrelevant. Here is that data from 1900-2016. US Large is VOO

62416552
Damn Breh just got a $2200 check do I just invest it boringly or do something fun with it

62417956
>>62416552
sweet

62418163
>>62414680
That's actually super comfy anon. I think I'll set up an account like that.

62419099
>ytd, 1 yr, 3 yr, 5 yr
My gains from picking a portfolio of 40-70 value stocks, ETFs, maybe 10% of portfolio in growth stocks which I cycle into something else whenever they run up a lot

I normally would agree with op but there’s a lot of research saying likely forward returns at these S&P500 multiples are going to be less than 2% a year

62419144
>>62401790
Perplexity Finance used several methodologies and concluded $1m in 2026 usd was approximately the entry level wealth for the equestrian class. Interesting

62419159
>>62409811
This. You should spend time and energy to increase your income. That will boost your savings rate.

62419709
>>62419099
What have you picked and what’s your philosophy
The reason I have been doing S&P ETFs is that it’s simple and I was just starting out
I basically just put my entire paycheck in there a week so it became an easy 2 min habit using an app

62419913
>>62419709
I follow smart people on x and read 13f statements from great investors. No grifters or gurus or people with mental illnesses, narcissism, or neuroses. Example: Cluseau Investments. This gives me most of my ideas these days because I've become a bit more lazy. I used to run screens on various sites for at least 10 hours a week. I also downloaded the entire stock ticker universe and then enriched the data with finnhub, Alpha Vantage, etc. I even did completely mong shit like having a python script take a screenshot of every known stock loaded into eval sites like GuruFocus etc and then simply counting the green pixel ratio.
But like I said I found I could just dumb this down to leech ideas from smart people who have already done this work.

99% of my buys need a PEG ratio below 1.2, forward p/e preferably cheap like below 13, and growth of both revenue and profit has to be up and to the right. Price to sales I'd also like to be low (I won't own spacex due to this).

Then I consider whether I think it will do well due to sector and if it's got a long base where it's gone nowhere. I like long bases where it's fallen out of favor because generally, everything will have its day again, plus I already protected myself quite a bit on the downside by needing low PEG ratios (either p/e is very low compared to growth or growth is very high compared to p/e). The only thing that can turn it into a disaster from this POV is if projected earnings are wildly wrong, or there is some kind of danger that turns a cheap 7 p/e stock into an absurdly cheap 3 p/e stock (those exist. I even own a 0.75 p/e serial acquirer from Germany that I lost $6k on). But I can afford a bunch of disasters per year because I have a big portfolio where I punt around 0.4% to 1.5% on each idea.

62419916
>>62419709
>>62419913
>continued due to length

>but what about ROIC, SBC, f-score, z-score etc
I used to meticulously track those too and avoid anything spooky, and I got good results. But now my final heuristic before pressing the order button is generally searching the ticker I want to buy on social media and seeing what other people see as particularly attractive or dangerous. If the stock has high SBC (stock based compensation) someone will always chime up about it and I avoid it

I've probably forgotten something, but tl;dr I've attacked stock selection from every angle and now I feel like I can just feel it out very quickly based on a couple dumb heuristics. If you have to do pretzel math to convince yourself the stock is a good idea, it's a worse idea than the good ideas you'll come across so why buy it.

62419932
>>62419709
>>62419916
That said, I also pile up a bunch of ETFs for additional diversification. People hate on diversification these days, and even Warren Buffett says derisively that diversification is protection from ignorance. I’m happy with that. Win in my book

62419947
What about things like DRAM and SOXX
Especially SOXX since it invests in the entirety of the AI infrastructure, wouldn't that be a big bet vs just going into the market

62419965
>>62419947
Semiconductors as a sector is basically guaranteed up and to the right over the long term but look at the chart right now. It would be completely unsurprising IF people who buy here after this insane pump are stuck in a negative position for many years, even a decade. Happens a lot

62419969
>>62419965
Yeah...I keep missing the boat lol.....and now it seems like Apple is asking to buy chinese memory chips so I wonder if Im lucky to not be in micron rn

62419980
By the way guys, here's a cool free tool to backtest ETF compositions:
https://www.portfoliovisualizer.com/backtest-portfolio#analysisResults

I forgot the exact ratio but I read that tech plus small caps combo is gold tier returns wise and it seemed to be true when I last played with this tool.

>>62419969
If you get frustrated it's best to just buy the whole market imho. You get better returns than most people (average retail investor only gets around 5% CAGR) and get to keep your time and peace of mind

62419989
>>62419980
im fine with doing this shit long term but do have slight fomo about all this stuff being a once in a lifetime money making opportunity....but i guess if that were true the residual "go up and right" should also last a while

62420075
>negative correlation between amount of text in a post and intelligence of that post

62420076
>>62420075
Youre gay lol

62420079
>>62420076
no u

62420460
>>62420079
owned

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1 — 167 replies — 24.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1

Open thread • Replies: 167 • Updated: 2026-06-27 14:11 UTC

OP

Brothers,

SpaceX has begun to rise. Its success or fall is of little importance to us in the short term.

In the meanwhile we must devise ways to gain a spot at the table with our kin in the six figure club.

I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.

However, I would be remiss to not tell the truth that I haven't a clue what we should study.

So instead I suggest we create an eggregore for these threads. Others made monero-chan, bobo and sminem

I am thinking we create one based on a girl who would be willing to believe in us while we are poor and brave the pain throughout having to witness our more disappointing moments of action. Never losing faith in us, she is always there. Yes. This is how we shall spend our time.

At any rate, that is my suggestion for tonight's fun. I have to work in the morning so let's not get too rowdy. Take it or leave it. If you will take it, then be sure to double it and pass it on to the next anon.

Let us commence the general!

Come one, come all!

Early replies (first 3)

62373772
>>62373752
Jannies will surely delete these threads. Instead of creating an eggregore why not try something new and usurp an old one. It may be a derivative of one which has already been made.

62373798
>>62373772
Hello anon thanks for joining. You are poster #1. Your anime image shall be the eggregore. The "coffee" in the image is rather a good omen. We all know images with hot girls accompanied by a brief post on coffee attracts all the attention.

Now that matter is settled, help me add some links to this post here. This will be the header for the start of each general

Helpful links

>Educational materials
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Looking to try the TCG route?
https://youtube.com/@alphainvestments69?si=tDAPjnRSZhyCIGP5

>Lost all hope?
https://www.indeed.com/


>Hidden in plain sight
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Limitless_(film)

>Games to strengthen you financial acumen
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Learn your from your betters
https://ia600609.us.archive.org/3/items/the-house-of-rothschild-ferguson-niall/The%20House%20of%20Rothschild%20-%20Ferguson%2C%20Niall.pdf
What is the name of that character in your post?

62373799
>>62373772
verily, a true fact

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62401809
>>62401765
I did it the hard way. Degree in finance. Work in finance. Almost 20 years. Been investing since the 08 crash. There's no quick trick or easy money. Crypto is bullshit. Just keep buying.

62401893
Six figures is truly hell, I miss being proud of 70k.

62403333
>>62401765
It was one art guy

62403337
>>62401765
This general also is a work in progress, I am hoping 10 generals from now we have a much better work flow, plan and ultimately success. In some capacity.

62404260
Bump

62404574
No point in this general. Fastest way to make 10k is just to work for a bit, you shouldn’t be investing

62404709
Bump

62404737
>>62373752
its a spacex thread?
its time to buy o better buy in few days one week

62405715
bump

62407354
Page 8 bump, /biz/ must be losing money again because there's an uptick in activity.

62407450
almost 9 months since i started my first job after college
this feels stagnant and joyless
what exactly do i need to do to escape wageslavery and poverty

62407931
Someone send me ten grand so I can join the club, thanks

62408558
>>62407450
What were you doing in 2017, 2021 and December of 2024?

62408565
>>62407354
Thank you, my wage job prevents me from dedicating my energies to this thread but I should have some time soon

62408611
>>62408584
Wtf are you on about dude, yes I made 54 posts over the span of 8 days. I'm surprised you're not taking your own advice and leaving 4chan.

62410044
Bump

62410051
Under 10k you just need to have a job and save cash.
You need cash flow and if you dont have shit a job is the only way to start.

62410731
Bump

62411520
>>62408558
>2017
still in high school
>2021
still in uni
>December of 2024
just graduated and was looking for jobs (i still had 0 dollars to my name at that point)

62411614
>>62373752
>>62373772
>>62373798
>>62373799
>>62373803
>post Maccari's "Cicero Denounces Catiline" (1888)
>crop out Catiline
Fuck you OP, I'm glad you're poor.

62412314
>>62411520
You could've played the memecoin casino in 2021.

62413842
Bump, I'll be free tomorrow

62416357
Bump

62417812
page 9 bump. Don't say I never did anything for you.

62418065
Almost the weekend then I can devote my energies to this thread.

And a big thank you to the poster above this post, looks like you were based after all

62418133
Bump

62418220
I got out and made tens of dollars this week with MU and SNDK. Lost some with WDC.

62419326
This is very stupid but an anon in some other thread weeks ago shilled me TNYA. He gave no explanation, no catalyst, no description what the company even does but he said that we’ll be eating good in like 1-2 years I don’t even remember exactly when. But hey it’s a cheap stock and he sounded confident to me

62419390
go all in hbar when it bottoms at 0.03

62419964
Bump, just woke up, let's get started

item.title: I receive 4k month from VA disability. I make 48k a year just sitting on my ass but I still want travel, get a gf, and b… — 12 replies — 23.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
I receive 4k month from VA disability. I make 48k a year just sitting on my ass but I still want travel, get a gf, and b…

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-06-27 20:28 UTC

OP

I receive 4k month from VA disability. I make 48k a year just sitting on my ass but I still want travel, get a gf, and be able to afford my hobbies what is the easiest shit i can do for an extra 20k?

Early replies (first 3)

62420624
you can travel everywhere you want with 4k usd per month

sorry for being disabled, kys

62420635
>>62420624
its a common VA scam, the only disability he seems to have is the mental retardadion due to lack of critical thinking

62420658
>>62420624
Yes I can but then I'll have to move out of my studio to save 1k. Id still like to have an HQ to return. I could live with my mommy but I dont feel like it. Also I told you want to get a gf id like a little extra and then just as bumper should i travel vlog too?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62420680
>>62420617
You americans are literally mentally disabled. You can travle the world for 2k a month. For 4k a month you can do luxury travel.

62420688
>move to beach town
>rent out jetskis or offer scuba diving lessons to tourists
>work whenever you feel like it
>have an endless stream of tourist pussy coming straight to you

Simple as

62420699
>>62420680
Give me logistics for how i should travel?

62420704
>>62420688
Jetskis are logistic nightmare and I'll have to go to diving school which I can't do anyway due health reasons currently. Tell me some other ideas nigga

62420707
>>62420699
>>62420704
Is this a ragebait thread? There's no way you're actually this helpless, lmao

62420727
>>62420707
Shut the fuck up nigga and answer my damn question

62420756
>>62420727
Proof with timestamp and I have a few good ideas.

62420766
>>62420727
Go to /trv/ if you want to learn how to travel on a budget. Specifically the SEA and South America threads. Stay out of Japan

62420770
>>62420699

item.title: Why aren't you holding BTC? — 95 replies — 19.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why aren't you holding BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 95 • Updated: 2026-06-27 16:09 UTC

OP

Too expensive? Prefer alts? Don't trust it? Or do you simply think there are better opportunities elsewhere?
Curious to hear your thoughts.

Early replies (first 3)

62395695
you can get better gains on stocks. those companies work for your money. btc sits there doing nothing but has the same risk profile as sharing a hobos needle without greater fools left to carry it to $200k

62395915
give me reasons to hold btc.

62395925
I am holding because I bought in 2020 before etfs now I refuse to sell because I'll get audit raped by the CRA supposedly so I'm stuck holding until it's got better tax laws or borrowing rates.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62414938
>>62414893
He knows what's he's doing

62414950
>>62413082
It won't work forever

62414955
>>62414944
How?

62415214
>>62414950
Hmmm, that's why it's still in public testnet instead of being pushed straight into production. I'm more interested in where it could lead than writing it off this early.

62415217
>>62414893
MSTR has definitely been a major buyer, but I don't think Bitcoin's entire value proposition depends on one company continuing to buy forever.

62415228
>>62395655
Because I have no brown, black or asian admixture.

62415296
>>62395655
Bitcorn has no liquidity now - there are no baggies left to trap.

I'd rather gamble on stocks - at least the price actually moves.

62415297
>>62396879
There is 'demand' for literally meme garbage like PEPE and DOGE which do nothing - not really an argument.

62415301
>>62415217
No, but MSTR collapse will cause a negative doom loop for Bitcorn if they have to start dumping.

62415492
>>62415228
Are you white?

62416132
>>62395655
I sold between 90-110k
Bought back between 59-64k
Still 1/3 of Tether left to buy further dips

62416334
>>62411213
Yeah. The Aegis partnership makes me even more interested to see how the TBV public testnet progresses.

62416338
>>62415301
but short term

62416355
>>62416132
Nice trades.

62416385
>>62395655
because I make money trading not holding garbage

62416439
>>62416385
Because I believe Bitcoin is the strongest long-term store of value in crypto. Short-term volatility doesn't change why I bought it in the first place.

62416488
>>62411265
I'm not holding bitcoin for next week. I'm holding it for the next decade.

62416497
>>62411213
I have doubts for Aave coin

62416506
Because every major dip has looked scary in the moment, but the long-term trend is what matters to me.

62416528
eistein coin

62417470
>>62416385
Garbage? Seriously?

62417484
>>62416497
Nobody's saying you have to like the coin. The point is the protocol and why it still dominates DeFi lending.

62417496
>>62411241
your choice

62417853
>>62415297
Memecoins are different

62419146
>>62395655
Because I'm not a retard

62419150
I know the maxis wont want to watch this…but btc is fundamentally flawed, as are all pow and pos consensus mechanisms…it’s not gonna work long term
https://m.youtube.com/watch?v=fo8ZScrXFZE&ra=m
Jesus loves you, Christ is King

62419226
>>62395655

62419255
>>62419226

62419275
>>62419255

62420185

62413573

So another exchange shutting down and stealing all users funds?

62414199
>>62413262
The ama was hard to watch. The amount of times he had to say he can't guarantee people's funds and that it's up to the courts was irritating. He kept saying they are all in it together and that he was too as in investor, ceo and user of the platform. I bet he didn't halt his own withdrawals though. What a fuckin goon on the whole "reorganization" pitch. Hopefully karma gets that stupid mother fucker. His body language said it all.

62414273
it's over. the sec need to be restored so it can go after every single piece of shit crypto project and exchange

62414281
>>62410743

62414293
>>62414281

62414317

62414659
>>62410743

62414772
imagine trusting a man with a hairline like that? couldnt be me

62414835
>>62410743
My 1 whole btc is on there. I procrastinated putting it my own wallet. Are they scaming or we can get it back?

62414898
How the fuck does an exchange go bankrupt without a huge hack? Its like Trump bankrupting a casino. It should not happen

62414931
>>62410743
tfw a personal friend of mine for years started a crypto coin that actually took off when musk mentioned it a few years ago. it mooned and he sold all he held to pay off his house. black lives matter-tier scammer. you literally cannot trust anyone.

62415834
>>62410743
>NO ONE is trustworthy in crypto
No one is trustworthy. Cursed is the man who trusts in man.

62415841

62416840

62419975

62419990
>>62410743

62419997
i made a cm account years ago because they were the first exchange to list a link-euro pair. i think it was only a matter of time until they went under, they have been struggling since day 1. no volume, no traders, no fees collected. i don't think cm was a fraudulent exchange, just a bad one. anyone keeping a significant amount on any exchange is a stupid retard, they go under all the time, especially in bear market times. you get what you fucking deserve.

62420399
>>62413165

item.title: Chinese Banker commits suicide via 9/11 style plane crash - allegedly due to a failed margin call — 32 replies — 9.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Chinese Banker commits suicide via 9/11 style plane crash - allegedly due to a failed margin call

Open thread • Replies: 32 • Updated: 2026-06-27 17:37 UTC

OP

>Top CITIC Bank executive Junhua "Sabrina" Liu (刘俊华), Client Management Assistant GM, reportedly lost everything in a devastating margin call.

>According to leaked QQ chats: 'She failed a margin call' 'Flew a plane to ram the CITIC building.'


https://x.com/i/status/2070653653795709229

https://www.nst.com.my/world/world/2026/06/1474324/small-plane-crashes-beijing-skyscraper-pilot-killed-13-injured-watch

https://x.com/i/status/2070656941714710587

Early replies (first 3)

62420228
>>62420225
Her pic

62420232
>>62420225
Linked in Profile

https://www.linkedin.com/in/俊华-刘-96b884a2

62420234
F

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62420239
women are pathetic attention whores

good that she is dead though

S

62420242
Ummm why didn't the building collapse?

62420247
>>62420242
Was a tiny plane. It's one of the tallest buildings in China so it's probably built pretty sturdy anyway.

62420248
>>62420242
Do you have an electronics or 3d modelling project you are working on recently? Are you using Tinkercad or SolidWorks?

62420251
>>62420239
Wouldn't a failed margin call in this particular case basically mean her life savings or whatever got wiped out? He might have also owed a debt so an-heroing what's a quick way to get out of it if she couldn't possibly pay it back.

62420254
>>62420225
Sky King failed at life, Sky Queen failed a margin call

62420256
>>62420242
are you retarded? flying a plane into a building doesn't cause it to collapse.

62420261
>>62420225
>spent her entire life climbing the ladder
>finally got to the upper floors
>made some floor shattering blunders
>couldn't comprehend the psychological implication of going back to the lower floors
>committed suicide in the most high IQ, eastern corporate cutthroat way possible

62420265
>>62420261
I mean finding out you're going to be a dead slave for life will probably psychologically fuck up even some of the most resilient people. I think it's a combination of not being able to handle it and suicide being literally the only way to get out of it. I just hope for the sake of anyone close to her the death doesn't somehow get shoved on to them.

62420267
>>62420225
Actually kino way to go out

62420278
>>62420225
Also should have mentioned the CCP went into overdrive trying to cover up the fact that it even happened. Didn't work for obvious reasons (I don't even know why even they would care to cover this up. One person fucked up and died. Why would they feel embarrassed about this?)

https://www.cnn.com/2026/06/27/china/beijing-plane-crash-citic-tower-censorship-china-intl-hnk

62420307
>>62420225
I applaud

62420346
>>62420242
She used an LSA like a retard

62420347
>>62420346
Newfag here. What's lsa?

62420350
>>62420242
It wasn't insured for terrorism

62420352
>>62420278
Maybe the woman is related to some upper echelon CCP official and he wants to save face?

62420353
>>62420242
There weren't people waiting to dance...

62420354
>>62420347
Light Sport Aircraft. Stupid easy to get licensed to fly one compared to a regular General Aviation plane

62420357
>>62420354
I don't get why that would be a bad aircraft to use if her intent was sewer-syde. Maybe she was just a casual flyer herself before her fuck up?

62420362
>>62420357
Because if she wanted to take down a building she wouldn't use one. An LSA is only dangerous to its pilot

62420365
>>62420362
Who says she was trying to take down the building though? Maybe it was just her way of saying "fuck you" without actually causing too much damage to innocent people

62420366
THEY FOUND HER PASSPORT ON THE STREET 2 MINUTES LATER

62420367
>>62420362
What plane would you have used then?

62420369
>>62420366
Citation pweaz

62420373
>>62420367
An airliner full of fuel.

62420376
>>62420256
Oy vey

62420377
>>62420225
who cares some chink killed herself? why do u niggers even give a shit there's like 1.4bn of them who even cares im sure the people on the ground there dont even give a fuck.

62420381
>>62420373
Pretty sure that's much harder to get. She was a "pilot" the same way a guy renting a speedboat can be considered a "sailor"

62420382
/biz/lets wouldn't even have the balls

item.title: >WHY DO THEY ALWAYS SET THE PAR? >WHY! DO! THEY ALWAYS SET THE PAR? >THEY ALWAYS SET THE PAR!! — 15 replies — 4.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
>WHY DO THEY ALWAYS SET THE PAR? >WHY! DO! THEY ALWAYS SET THE PAR? >THEY ALWAYS SET THE PAR!!

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-06-27 17:45 UTC

OP

>WHY DO THEY ALWAYS SET THE PAR?
>WHY! DO! THEY ALWAYS SET THE PAR?
>THEY ALWAYS SET THE PAR!!

Early replies (first 3)

62418248
>>62418238
>GROWING PROFITS DISSAPEARING INTO THE MARKETS DRY MOUTH

62418271
>>62418238
Saylor is such a retard. His old ponzi was already bad but to top it off with an ai made tera usd 2.0 is clinically retarded.

62418319
>people worried about institutions buying messing up the cycle
kek. This is how we get to 25k. Saylor's going to lose control of his company and some idiot's going to dump all his holdings on the open market.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62418323
>>62418238
25k? More like you'll have to pay tens of thousands of BTC for a pizza again.

62418351
>>62418323
yeah sooner then later nobody wants the funny internet tokens, good for nothing.

62418443
>>62418351
that's right
sell them all now or be poor forever and ever

62418457
>>62418238
you could have made ungodly money selling puts on this piece of shit before it fell off a cliff

62418461
>>62418457
oops i meant buying puts

62418488
The yield is currently over 15% btw

62418497
>>62418238
$50 soon

62418548
>>62418488
>3% increase vs 8% drop
They’re gonna wait until it crashes to $1 before retiring it kek.

62418561
>>62418488
we finna be rich

62418587
>>62418488
Plus it'll be $100 soon so you make like 25% on top of it.

No way this is just going to crash and burn

62420398
>>62418319
The price of bitcoin has been controlled by institutions for a long time now anon..

62420406
>>62418488
